article.body_preview:
DHAKA, Feb 13, 2023 (BSS) - To support export and import-based businesses to

cope with global uncertainties brought on by the COVID-19 pandemic, IFC is

providing $50 million to Prime Bank Limited in Bangladesh.

This IFC funding will support working capital, trade finance, and foreign

exchange liquidity needs of export and import based entities borrowing

through Prime Bank's Offshore Banking Unit.

The financing package is part of IFC's 'Fast Track COVID-19 Facility,' a

package designed to

llm_connections.evidence_quote: This IFC funding will support working capital, trade finance, and foreign exchange liquidity needs of export and import based entities
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly discusses import-based entities and their trade finance needs, which matches the feature’s country-level concept of imports as goods brought in.

verification.quote_best_match: This IFC funding will support working capital, trade finance, and foreign exchange liquidity needs of export and import based entities
verification.judge_reasoning: The quoted text appears verbatim in the article. The article explicitly says the funding will support working capital, trade finance, and foreign exchange liquidity needs of export and import-based entities, which directly implies support for import financing. The discussion is at the broader Bangladesh trade-finance/market level, not just a single-company level.
